rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH DIANE SCHENK VS. ELIZABETH JANE MCCARTHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 27, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED ANSWER is continued to July 17, 2023 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 57 TAYLOR I7, LP VS. FRANCISCO JUARES HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 27, 2023 line 9. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT Hearing required to address whether mandatory relief is being sought. If Defendant is seeking mandatory relief, Defendant's counsel shall file a declaration no later than 8:30 a.m. on June 27, 2023 supporting the request. Declaration must be in the proper form and all the requirements of the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court must be met. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: YUE KEUNG YEUNG VS. OLENA KATS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 27, 2023 line 10. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Defendants failed TO shift their burden and failed to show that they are entitled to a judgment as a matter of law: (1) no authority is cited for the proposition that the operative notice does not support a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on the washer/dryer violation alone; and (2) no analysis is provided for the conclusory argument that Plaintiff admitted that defendants cured the violation and the timing of such cure, i.e. no authority or argument is made as to the timing of such cure - within the cure period; after the cure period, etc. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NORTHRIDGE COOPERATIVE HOMES, INC. VS. DOES 1 TO 50, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 27, 2023 line 11. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTIANED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege each element of the causes of action for forcible entry and forcible detainer. Plaintiff shall separate the claims into separate causes of action (the cause of action is currently labeled "forcible entry & detainer - CCP 1161(4)," referencing possibly three distinct claims governed by 3 separate statutes with different elements and requirements). Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Any tenancy claims and protections afforded to tenants raise a factual dispute challenging the allegations in the Complaint asserting that the defendant is not a tenant. The Court notes that the motion papers are not in conformity with the formatting California Rules of Court. Non-conforming papers will be stricken and will not be considered in the future. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
